title: EIP-7701: Native Account Abstraction
description: Introduces native account abstraction by adding a new transaction type that splits processing into validation, execution, and post-operation steps. Separates authorization from gas payment, allowing paymasters to sponsor gas for other accounts. Adds new opcodes (CURRENT_ROLE, ACCEPT_ROLE, TXPARAM*) for contracts to participate in structured transaction lifecycle roles.
author: Vitalik Buterin (@vbuterin), Yoav Weiss (@yoavw), Alex Forshtat (@forshtat), Dror Tirosh (@drortirosh), Shahaf Nacson (@shahafn)
laymanDescription: Lets Ethereum smart contract wallets define their own rules for approving transactions and paying gas fees. A separate account (called a paymaster) can cover gas costs on behalf of users, removing the need to hold ETH just to transact. This enables more flexible wallet designs and simpler onboarding.

endUsers.description: Enables smart contract wallets with custom authentication and sponsored transactions, removing the requirement to hold ETH for gas fees.

appDevs.description: Unlocks paymaster-sponsored transactions and flexible account logic, enabling gasless onboarding flows and new wallet interaction patterns.

walletDevs.description: Must integrate the new AA transaction type and role-based validation flow; gains ability to build wallets with custom signing and recovery schemes.

toolingInfra.description: Block explorers, indexers, and debuggers must support the new transaction type, opcodes, and multi-step execution trace format.

layer2s.description: Layer 2s already using ERC-4337 may align with native AA semantics; rollups processing EVM transactions must support the new transaction type.

stakersNodes.description: Validators must process the new transaction type with its multi-phase execution; mempool handling requires updated validation logic.

clClients.description: Minimal direct impact; consensus clients need fork-activation configuration but no new beacon chain logic.

elClients.description: Significant implementation effort: new transaction type, five new opcodes, multi-phase execution pipeline, and updated mempool validation rules.

benefits: Enables native smart contract wallets with custom authentication and key management.
Allows paymasters to sponsor gas fees, removing ETH balance requirements for users.
Separates validation from execution, improving transaction processing flexibility.
Provides a protocol-level foundation replacing off-chain account abstraction workarounds.
tradeoffs: Adds EVM complexity with five new opcodes and a multi-phase transaction pipeline.
Security-critical ACCEPT_ROLE opcode requires careful auditing of all deployed contracts.
Mempool validation becomes more complex, requiring execution of custom validation code.

improveUX.description: Directly improves user experience by enabling smart contract wallets with custom authentication, social recovery, and gasless transactions via paymasters.
